creator: ports@grosbein.net
text:
> I really wouldn't want to include manual fetching-and-patching of the
> mplayer sources if it is not really necessary and an important feature
> for many users.

It should not be necessary to maintain this addition.
You can read some details from comments of the beginning
of libavcodec/amr.c. There is no patching of mplayer sources;
really this is how AMR support must be compiled in accordingly
official mplayer's directions.

It seems for me that demand for 3gp support is growing,
at least Google shows lots of user questions about mplayer & AMR topic,
I've found them while looking for solution when the problem faced.

> Also (I am unfamiliar with the AMR stuff), there is a
> vast number of different files/series/versions/patches/tarballs on this
> 3GPP ftp. This tarball you picked, I suppose you had specific reasons?

Yes. There are ports audio/libamrnb and audio/libamrwb that refer to
these tarballs. Perhaps, it's possible to force mplayer to use these
ports but that would involve heavy patching of mplayer source without
any real benefit. So I've decided to follow directions from amr.c
and that gives excellent results.

> Is this a particular patch to the ffmpeg version that comes with
> mplayer rc1? What about mplayer rc2? Will these patches apply as well?

They should. By the way, currently amr.c refers to older version
of tarballs but newer work just fine. It seems that API it well declared.
Again, there is no patching of mplayer sources really. Mplayer's configure
script just looks if there are sources for AMR codecs in place;
they are missing by default due to licensing issues but may be
fetched separately. So the patch does it - fetches (really bsd.port.mk does),
unpacks and places symlinks so that configure would find codec sources.

> I have to admit I am a bit sceptical that we need this at the moment,
> but maybe if I knew more details...

Please read the beginnging of amr.c I've pointed at.

Eugene Grosbein
